methods.description: Process for collecting freshwater sponges from the Paraná River using scuba-diving techniques.

steps.description: We looked for freshwater sponges in the Paraná River by scuba-diving at depths ranging from 0.5 m to 35 m.
steps.searchable_summary: search and collect freshwater sponges from river using scuba-diving

steps.description: We searched for sponges in several potential substrates, such as decaying wood, rocks, and macrophytes.
steps.searchable_summary: identify potential substrates for sponge collection

methods.description: Procedure for rearing chironomid larvae from collected sponges and identifying them to the lowest taxonomic level.

steps.description: When found, the sponges were taken to the laboratory and, when possible, they were kept in an oxygenated aquarium in order to rear the chironomid larvae to obtain adults.
steps.searchable_summary: rear chironomid larvae in laboratory aquarium

steps.description: The larvae were sorted using a stereomicroscope, mounted on slides and identified to the lowest taxonomic level possible given the limited knowledge of the Neotropical fauna in this group.
steps.searchable_summary: sort and identify chironomid larvae using stereomicroscope

methods.description: Analysis of the ecological relationship between chironomids and freshwater sponges using four specific criteria.

steps.description: Four criteria were used to analyze the ecological relation between the chironomids and the freshwater sponges: 1) gut content, 2) morphology of the larvae, 3) the occurrence of the larvae inside the sponges, and 4) the restricted occurrence of larvae in sponges.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze ecological relationship using specified criteria

methods.description: Procedure for mounting chironomid larvae on slides and measuring larval head capsule size.

steps.searchable_summary: mount chironomid larvae on slides with Euparal

steps.description: Larval head capsule size is given as the 'ventral head length' from the tip of the mentum to the postoccipital margin.
